Would not run until PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command% is used in the launch options
title: Assassin's Creed™: Director's Cut Edition

notes: Works after adding PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command% to launch options. Alt+Tab doesn't work, it closes the game.
title: Assassin's Creed™: Director's Cut Edition

notes:
Meanwhile Steam Proton team is working to resolve this issue, this is others workarounds:

    Play directx9 version of AS
    Instead of renaming files, there is a easier workaround: right click on the game on your Steam library, "properties", "game launch options" and add this line:
    PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%
    this option will disable completely Wine directx10 and 11 availability, the game will now launch automatically in directx9 mode.

    Play the Directx10 version
    Launch the game once time, it will crash but it stored Proton game profile files.
    Then download DXVK release from Github:
    https://github.com/doitsujin/dxvk/releases/
    extract the archive, open a terminal, place you in the folder where is stored the file "setup_dxvk.verb" and execute this command:
    WINEPREFIX="/home/your_profil/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/15100/pfx" winetricks d3dcompiler_43 setup_dxvk.verb
    The game now launch with Directx10 mode.
    To be sure, add this line to the Launch Steam game option:
    DXVK_HUD=version,devinfo,fps %command%
title: Assassin's Creed™: Director's Cut Edition

notes: Opens a window and immediately closes
title: Assassin's Creed™: Director's Cut Edition

notes:
Runs flawlessly with DirectX 10 mode once use you use protontricks script to override  the d3d_compiler43.dll, since DirectX 10 emulation on DXVK requires to replace the Wine's d3d_compiler43.dll in order to work.
Once downloaded protontricks and winetricks, type the following command on the shell:
[$ python3 protontricks 15100 d3dcompiler_43 ].

On my system, the open world scenario runs between 90 to 150 fps at FullHD (1080p), where on some close areas it can increase up to 200 fps.
 During Desmon Miles wanderings it runs the scenario at 300 fps.

When I once tested the DirectX 9 (disable D3d11), the performance are not so great, and runs less than 60 fps.
title: Assassin's Creed™: Director's Cut Edition

notes: First I have tried to disable D3D11, with PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command% set in Launch Options, but I got pretty low performance. Game ran in outside areas around 20-30FPS, but as I looked into ground FPS jumped up to 200FPS. Then I tried to use Wine D3D11, with the help of protontricks. I ran this: protontricks 15100 d3dcompiler_43 - and then launched the game without PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command% and performance improved very well. Now I get around 80-110FPS in open areas. Plays smooth with minor shuttering which seems to be related to audio.
title: Assassin's Creed™: Director's Cut Edition

notes: Works great, but not with DXVK on Vulkan, I had to set the parameter PROTON_NO_D3D11=1.
